answer.text: Government spending more and taxing less to stimulate the economy
Government spending less and taxing more to cool the economy

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Expansionary fiscal policy = government spends more and taxes less, keeping more money with citizens and corporates to expand the economy. This results in fiscal deficits. **The Trap:** Spending less and taxing more is contractionary fiscal policy. Raising rates and reducing money supply are monetary, not fiscal, measures. **Cross-Exam Context:** Fiscal policy types tested in NISM Series XV and I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Expansionary fiscal policy during recessions follows Keynesian economics. The stimulus package during COVID-19 was an example.
